Package com.hummeling.if97

Class IF97

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hummeling.if97.IF97
  • public class IF97
extends Object

    Steam tables for industrial use according to the international standard for the properties of water and steam, the IAPWS-IF97 formulation and the international standards for transport and other properties.

    By default, units are as given in the book cited below. See IF97.UnitSystem for options.

    Properties are generally available as functions of three quantity combinations: pressure & temperature (p, T), pressure & specific enthalpy (p, h), and specific enthalpy & specific entropy (h, s).

    • Wagner, Wolfgang & Kretzschmar, Hans-Joachim, 2008, International Steam Tables — Properties of Water and Steam Based on the Industrial Formulation IAPWS-IF97, 2nd Edition, Springer-Verlag, Berlin Heidelberg, ISBN 978-3-540-21419-9
    Website: www.if97.software.
    Author:
    Ralph Hummeling (www.hummeling.com)

      • densityHS

        public double densityHS​(double enthalpy,
                        double entropy)
                 throws OutOfRangeException
        Density as a function of specific enthalpy & specific entropy.

        This is a convenience method which simply calls 1.0 / specificVolumeHS(enthalpy, entropy).

        Parameters:
        enthalpy - specific enthalpy
        entropy - specific entropy
        Returns:
        density
        Throws:
        OutOfRangeException - out-of-range exception
        See Also:
        specificVolumeHS(double, double)

      • partialDerivativePH

        public double partialDerivativePH​(double pressure,
                                  double enthalpy,
                                  IF97.Quantity x,
                                  IF97.Quantity y,
                                  IF97.Quantity z)
                           throws OutOfRangeException
        Partial derivative of z with respect to x for constant y in SI units, as a function of pressure and specific enthalpy.

        (∂z/∂x)y(p, h)

        Parameters:
        pressure - absolute pressure
        enthalpy - specific enthalpy
        x - any IF97.Quantity part of the set returned by IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives()
        y - any IF97.Quantity part of the set returned by IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives()
        z - any IF97.Quantity part of the set returned by IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives()
        Returns:
        partial derivative [SI units]
        Throws:
        OutOfRangeException - out-of-range exception
        See Also:
        IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives()

      • partialDerivativePT

        public double partialDerivativePT​(double pressure,
                                  double temperature,
                                  IF97.Quantity x,
                                  IF97.Quantity y,
                                  IF97.Quantity z)
                           throws OutOfRangeException
        Gets the partial derivative of z with respect to x for constant y in SI units, as a function of pressure and temperature.

        (∂z/∂x)y(p, T)

        Note that this method is not suitable for the saturated region as pressure and temperature are coupled. Preferably, use partialDerivativePH(double, double, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity) instead.
        Parameters:
        pressure - absolute pressure
        temperature - temperature
        x - any IF97.Quantity part of the set returned by IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives()
        y - any IF97.Quantity part of the set returned by IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives()
        z - any IF97.Quantity part of the set returned by IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives()
        Returns:
        partial derivative [SI units]
        Throws:
        OutOfRangeException - out-of-range exception
        See Also:
        IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives(), partialDerivativePH(double, double, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity)

      • partialDerivativeRhoT

        public double partialDerivativeRhoT​(double density,
                                    double temperature,
                                    IF97.Quantity x,
                                    IF97.Quantity y,
                                    IF97.Quantity z)
                             throws OutOfRangeException
        Gets the partial derivative of z with respect to x for constant y in SI units, as a function of density and temperature, valid in region 3 only!

        (∂z/∂x)y(ρ, T)

        Preferably, use partialDerivativePH(double, double, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity) instead.
        Parameters:
        density - density
        temperature - temperature
        x - any IF97.Quantity part of the set returned by IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives()
        y - any IF97.Quantity part of the set returned by IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives()
        z - any IF97.Quantity part of the set returned by IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives()
        Returns:
        partial derivative [SI units]
        Throws:
        OutOfRangeException - out-of-range exception
        See Also:
        IF97.Quantity.getPartialDerivatives(), partialDerivativePH(double, double, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity, com.hummeling.if97.IF97.Quantity)
